documents.snippet: Annual leave requests must be submitted at least three working days before the planned absence. Manager approval is required before the leave is valid.
Travel reimbursement must include invoices and itinerary records. Amounts above 5000 require an additional department head approval.
The weekly project report must include progress this week, risks and blockers, next week plan, and cross-team coordination requests.
Scanned PDFs first try native text extraction. If readable text is insufficient, OCR is used and page-level text is preserved.
Before release, the team must complete security review, permission audit, database backup, rollback plan, and smoke testing.
For high severity incidents, rollback must be initiated within fifteen minutes when the error budget is exhausted or data corruption is suspected.
RAG quality is measured by hit rate at one, hit rate at top k, citation accuracy, weak evidence ratio, and average retrieval latency.
MCP tools must enforce user identity, project permission checks, dry run for write operations, confirmation before apply, and full audit logging.
Deleted project files are retained for thirty days before permanent removal. Audit logs are retained for at least one year.
The development environment is started through Docker Compose. The AI service, backend, database, Redis, Qdrant, MinIO, UI, and Nginx run as containers.

cases.query: How many working days in advance should annual leave be submitted?
cases.referenceAnswer: Annual leave requests must be submitted at least three working days before the planned absence.

cases.query: Who must approve leave before it is valid?
cases.referenceAnswer: Manager approval is required before leave is valid.

cases.query: What documents are required for travel reimbursement?
cases.referenceAnswer: Travel reimbursement must include invoices and itinerary records.

cases.query: When does reimbursement need department head approval?
cases.referenceAnswer: Amounts above 5000 require additional department head approval.

cases.query: What sections should the weekly project report include?
cases.referenceAnswer: It should include progress this week, risks and blockers, next week plan, and coordination requests.

cases.query: Does the weekly report template ask for risks and blockers?
cases.referenceAnswer: Yes, risks and blockers are required sections.

cases.query: What happens before OCR is used for scanned PDFs?
cases.referenceAnswer: The system first tries native text extraction.

cases.query: Does OCR preserve page-level text?
cases.referenceAnswer: Yes, OCR output preserves page-level text.

cases.query: What security checks are required before release?
cases.referenceAnswer: Security review and permission audit are required before release.

cases.query: Is a rollback plan part of the release checklist?
cases.referenceAnswer: Yes, a rollback plan is part of the release checklist.

cases.query: How quickly should rollback start for high severity incidents?
cases.referenceAnswer: Rollback should be initiated within fifteen minutes.

cases.query: When is rollback required if data corruption is suspected?
cases.referenceAnswer: Rollback is required when data corruption is suspected.

cases.query: Which metrics are used to measure RAG quality?
cases.referenceAnswer: RAG quality uses hit rate at one, hit rate at top k, citation accuracy, weak evidence ratio, and average latency.

cases.query: Does the RAG evaluation include retrieval latency?
cases.referenceAnswer: Yes, average retrieval latency is measured.

cases.query: What identity controls must MCP tools enforce?
cases.referenceAnswer: MCP tools must enforce user identity and project permission checks.

cases.query: What is required before MCP write operations are applied?
cases.referenceAnswer: Write operations require dry run, confirmation before apply, and audit logging.

cases.query: How long are deleted project files retained?
cases.referenceAnswer: Deleted project files are retained for thirty days.

cases.query: How long are audit logs retained?
cases.referenceAnswer: Audit logs are retained for at least one year.

cases.query: Which services run in the Docker development environment?
cases.referenceAnswer: The AI service, backend, database, Redis, Qdrant, MinIO, UI, and Nginx run as containers.

cases.query: How is the development environment started?
cases.referenceAnswer: It is started through Docker Compose.

cases.query: Before launch, what security and rollback items must be ready?
cases.referenceAnswer: Security review, permission audit, database backup, rollback plan, and smoke testing must be ready.

cases.query: Explain approval requirements for expenses over 5000.
cases.referenceAnswer: Expenses over 5000 require additional department head approval.

cases.query: What should a project status report cover next week?
cases.referenceAnswer: It should cover the next week plan and coordination requests.

cases.query: Where is permission audit mentioned as mandatory?
cases.referenceAnswer: Permission audit is mandatory before release and MCP tools must enforce project permission checks.

cases.query: Which evaluation metric checks whether references are correct?
cases.referenceAnswer: Citation accuracy checks whether references are correct.

cases.query: What metric tracks weak evidence in RAG results?
cases.referenceAnswer: Weak evidence ratio tracks weak evidence in RAG results.

cases.query: When does scanned PDF processing use OCR?
cases.referenceAnswer: OCR is used when native text extraction has insufficient readable text.

cases.query: Compare deleted file retention and audit log retention.
cases.referenceAnswer: Deleted files are retained for thirty days, while audit logs are retained for at least one year.

cases.query: Is Qdrant part of the Docker AI stack?
cases.referenceAnswer: Yes, Qdrant runs as a container in the Docker development environment.

cases.query: Do MCP tools require audit logging?
cases.referenceAnswer: Yes, MCP tools require full audit logging.
